title: Buttery Silky American Chicken Bisque
description: A rich, creamy, and diabetic-friendly American-inspired soup featuring tender chicken, buttery undertones, and a sprinkle of chopped chive. This dish is perfect for a comforting meal or a special occasion.
instructions: Heat the butter and olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the diced onion, celery, and carrots, and sauté until the vegetables are tender, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and sauté for 1 minute.
Add the chicken and sauté until browned, about 5-6 minutes.
Sprinkle the gluten-free flour over the chicken and vegetables, and cook for 1 minute.
Gradually add the chicken broth, whisking continuously to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-12 minutes.
Use an immersion blender or a regular blender to puree the soup until smooth.
Stir in the heavy cream and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Tadka: Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a small saucepan over medium heat. Add 1 tablespoon of chopped chives and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant. Stir the tadka into the bisque.
Serve hot, garnished with additional chopped chives if desired.
